t_ch: 老板送我的，我面子薄没多要。
t_en: The stallholder gave me this for free, and I'd feel embarrassed asking for more.

t_ch: 其实我也面子薄。
t_en: Yeah, I would feel bad asking for more, too.

t_ch: 这份是我的，你的要自己去买。
t_en: This one's mine. Now you can go buy your own.
...Hmph!

t_ch: 哎哟，这不是龙女大人嘛…来来来，我再给你们拿一些，千万不要客气。
t_en: Ah! It's the Dragon Lady... Here, I'll get you another serving — no need to be shy.

t_ch: 谢谢高阿姨！
t_en: Thank you, Auntie!

t_ch: 在食客们喧闹的交谈声中，时间就在不经意间匆匆逝去。
t_en: Amidst the bustling of the diners, time flies by before you even notice.

t_ch: 回过神来，你已经沉浸在金人巷的夜市气氛之中了。
t_en: When you return to your senses again, you find yourself immersed in the atmosphere of Aurum Alley's bustling night market.

t_ch: 有什么好吃的吗？
t_en: Is there anything good?

t_ch: （取出小吃）
t_en: (Take out the snack)

t_ch: 苏打豆汁儿…你、你不会是故意的吧？
t_en: Mung bean soda... Is this some sort of prank?

t_ch: 苏打豆汁儿，仙舟老饕都说妙！
t_en: The foodies here all say it's a must-try!

t_ch: 我是故意不小心的…
t_en: Tee-hee, I chose it on purpose~ *giggles proudly*

t_ch: …这一碗苏打豆汁儿还是你喝了吧。
t_en: Okay — you drink it then.

t_ch: 你喝吧，我不饿。
t_en: No, thanks, I'm not really thirsty.

t_ch: 不不不，你是外来的客人，你应该试试。
t_en: No, no, no, you're our honored guest — you can drink it.

t_ch: 我喝就我喝，这能有多可怕？
t_en: Fine. How bad can it be, anyway?

t_ch: 就像喝药一样，眼睛一闭心一横就喝完啦。
t_en: It's just like drinking medicine — close your eyes and cross your heart.

t_ch: 哎哟，这不是龙女大人嘛…来来来，我再给你们拿一些，千万不要客气。
t_en: Ah! It's the Dragon Lady... Here, I'll get you another serving — no need to be shy.

t_ch: 哎！豆汁？呃，不、不用了！唉，好吧。谢谢高阿姨。
t_en: Huh!? W—Wait, Auntie, I—I'm not thirsty, please... *sigh* Thank you, Auntie...

t_ch: 在食客们喧闹的交谈声中，你们随意地谈起有趣的话题。时间就在不经意间匆匆逝去，回过神来，你已经沉浸在金人巷的夜市气氛之中了。
t_en: Amidst the bustling of the night market, you and Bailu share news and fun stories. Time flies by before you even notice.

t_ch: 我差点就在小吃摊上吃饱了，正事还没有做呢。
t_en: *sigh* I'm so full of snacks I can hardly move. I haven't even gotten around to business yet.

t_ch: 这次出门想去买些药罐子，就在前面的「清玩斋」，走吧走吧。
t_en: I wanted to stop by Elegance Pagoda to buy some medicine jars while I'm out. Come on, let's go.

t_ch: 你们好好说话，可别欺负人——
t_en: Watch your words, no need to be aggressive here...

t_ch: 哟，码头出什么事啦？
t_en: Hey, what's going on at the dock?

t_ch: 公司的人和码头工人们吵起来了。
t_en: Some of the dock workers got into an argument with the IPC folks.

t_ch: …要不？我们先到码头上去看看吧。
t_en: ...How about we go see what's happening at the dock?

t_ch: 我就喜欢凑热闹。
t_en: Sounds exciting.

t_ch: 多一事不如少一事。
t_en: Sounds like trouble...

t_ch: 万一有人受伤了怎么办，快走。
t_en: What if someone gets hurt and needs medical attention? Come on.

t_ch: 喂，这又是从哪里蹿出来的野丫头，这关你什么事？
t_en: When did you decide to show up, little miss feisty? How is this any of your business?

t_ch: 身为云骑，码头上发生冲突，我岂有坐视不管的道理？还有，我不叫喂，也不叫野丫头，我叫素裳！
t_en: As a Cloud Knight, I can't just sit back and do nothing when I see civil unrest. And for your information, my name isn't "little miss feisty" — it's Sushang!

s_en: Person Appearing as Merchant Guild Secretary
t_ch: 姑娘，这件事就交给我们金人巷商会来处理吧。
t_en: Please, miss, allow Aurum Alley Merchant Guild to take care of this matter.

t_ch: 你们是…商会的？
t_en: You're from... the Merchant Guild?

t_ch: 你们来处理？真是笑死人了！
t_en: What a joke! You people can't "take care" of anything!

t_ch: 在这里围观的有一个算一个，谁不知道金人巷商会管理无方、经营无能。如今这码头租金积欠不还，你说这得怪谁？
t_en: Everyone here knows that the Aurum Alley Merchant Guild is as incompetent as it is dysfunctional. The lease for the docks isn't even paid yet — anyone care to guess whose fault that is...?

t_ch: ——这都要怪你们金人巷商会呀。
t_en: I'll tell you —the Merchant Guild's!

s_en: Person Appearing as Merchant Guild Secretary
t_ch: …这我并不否认。
t_en: ...I'm not denying that.

t_ch: 那你说大声一点呀，就说「金人巷商会对不起这里的商铺，把码头的事情搞砸了，金人巷商会的人全都是废物！」
t_en: Then say it out loud so everyone can hear: Aurum Alley Merchant Guild apologizes to every vendor here for defaulting on the lease and leaving us to pick up the mess!

t_ch: 然后为拖欠租金的日期数字给我响响亮亮地磕头，向代表公司的我道歉啊！
t_en: Better yet — get down on your knees and beg for forgiveness!

t_ch: 这话说的太难听了。
t_en: This guy sucks!!!

t_ch: 就是就是。
t_en: I agree.

t_ch: （继续看戏）
t_en: (Keep watching the drama)

t_ch: （抡起胳膊，准备干架）
t_en: (Prepare to beat this guy up.)

t_ch: 等等，我俩不是围观群众吗？
t_en: Wait — easy! We just came to watch!

t_ch: 喂，你态度也太嚣张了吧。积欠租金的事，好好商量不行吗？
t_en: Ugh, you're being completely unreasonable. Why can't we talk this over?

t_ch: 我也不是欺负人呀，我这不是友好地提醒一下吗，请问公司能回收租金吗？
t_en: I'm not being unreasonable, I'm making a friendly inquiry — when will the IPC receive its lease payments?

s_en: Person Appearing as Merchant Guild Secretary
t_ch: …现在不行。
t_en: ...I'm afraid we can't pay at the moment.

t_ch: 公司将这片码头的经营权转租给了你们商会，希望金人巷从此飞黄腾达…你看看，这么好的货运码头全给浪费了！
t_en: We leased this whole dock to your Merchant Guild hoping that it would rejuvenate Aurum Alley, and look at it now — a perfectly good freight dock has gone to waste!

t_ch: 谁说商会就一定还不上，这要…要是能还上呢？
t_en: They said "at the moment." That doesn't mean they'll never make the payments... right?

t_ch: 哎哟，我都不敢想。
t_en: Haha, don't be so gullible.

t_ch: 这样吧，咱俩打一个赌，要是商会能还上公司的租金，我就当着金人巷父老乡亲的面低头道歉……
t_en: How about we make a little bet — if the Merchant Guild can pay back the money it owes, I'll get down on my knees and apologize to all the people of Aurum Alley...

t_ch: 我还要大声地说「我是个有眼无珠的蠢货！不知天高地厚，只会狺狺狂吠！」然后我在你面前只能学狗叫，如何？
t_en: I'll shout it loud and clear — "I'm a raving idiot howling into the wind!" And then I'll bark like a dog. How does that sound?

t_ch: 好啊，要是我打赌输了怎么办？
t_en: Okay... and if they can't?

t_ch: 简单呀，你也当着父老乡亲的面如此向我赔礼道歉，在我的面前学狗叫。
t_en: Oh, that's simple. You apologize to me in front of all the people of Aurum Alley, and you bark like a dog.

t_ch: 别答应，这也太幼稚了。
t_en: This is so childish. Don't accept.

t_ch: 就是就是。
t_en: I agree.

t_ch: （继续看戏）
t_en: (Keep watching the drama)

t_ch: 怎么办啊？
t_en: Psst, what should she do?

t_ch: 答应他！
t_en: Take the bet!

t_ch: …你真是看热闹不嫌事大。
t_en: ...You're not being very helpful, are you?

t_ch: 一言为定！
t_en: Hmph! Deal!

t_ch: 大家都听到了，我就等着了，哈哈哈哈，我们走！
t_en: You all heard that, right? I'll be waiting for you, hahaha. Come on, let's get out of here!

s_en: Person Appearing as Merchant Guild Secretary
t_ch: 小姑娘，这样的赌约对你一点好处都没有…是我们连累了你。
t_en: Miss, there's no reason for you to take such a bet... we're the ones who dragged you into this.

t_ch: 没事没事，我看他盛气凌人的样子就觉得不爽，总该是要做点什么，要我袖手旁观可做不到。
t_en: It's okay — seeing that arrogant sneer on his face made me sick to my stomach. I had to do something.

t_ch: 姑娘的好意，我们又岂能不知，可就是……
t_en: We appreciate you sticking up for us, but this...

t_ch: 仗义执言，真了不起！
t_en: Standing up for what is right, mad respect!

t_ch: …冷、冷静下来，才发现要是输了会给云骑军丢脸。但是我也实在瞧不过那家伙的气焰！
t_en: ...Now that I think about it, if I lose the bet, it'll reflect badly on the Cloud Knights... but you can't give in to bullies!

t_ch: 你看你都卷进什么麻烦里了？
t_en: You just got yourself into trouble...

t_ch: …冷、冷静下来，才发现要是输了会给云骑军丢脸。但是你瞧那家伙气焰嚣张的样子，他要是败了学狗叫，那狼狈样子一定很有趣。
t_en: ...Now that I think about it, if I lose the bet, it'll reflect badly on the Cloud Knights... but you can't give in to bullies! He's the one who'll be barking like a dog.

t_ch: 商业问题还得让商会解决吧…
t_en: Merchant matters should be left to the Merchant Guild to handle...

t_ch: 这倒是没错，但是我也实在瞧不过那家伙的气焰嘛！
t_en: Maybe... but you can't give in to bullies!

t_ch: 我叫明曦，是金人巷商会的秘书，大家平时叫我「小秘书」。这金人巷码头的事，原本是商会与公司之间的纠纷，不该牵连各位的。
t_en: My name is Mingxi. I'm the secretary of Aurum Alley Merchant Guild — most people just call me Miss Secretary. This leasing dispute is between the IPC and the Merchant Guild — you shouldn't have been dragged into it.

t_ch: 再说这样的话可就疏远了。
t_en: I think it's a little late for that.

t_ch: 对呀，有什么「不该」的，我已经被牵连了。
t_en: Sure is, I've already started placing bets.

t_ch: 早知道我就不会插手了。
t_en: If I knew it'd come to this, I wouldn't have gotten involved.

t_ch: 明明和公司代表打赌的是我，你也没插手啊……
t_en: It was me who accepted the bet. You're not involved at all...

t_ch: 所以，码头真的还不上租金了吗？
t_en: So, is it true that the Merchant Guild can't pay back the lease?

t_ch: …小秘书，你就直说吧。
t_en: ...Miss Secretary, just tell it like it is.

t_ch: 按照现在的情况来看，金人巷码头经营不善，暂时还不上租金。
t_en: As things stand now, the Merchant Guild is in no position to pay back the lease.

t_ch: 这码头是商会租用的吗？
t_en: This dock was leased out to the Merchant Guild?

t_ch: 对，光是租用港口，商会就已经欠公司一屁股债了。
t_en: Yes, the entire port... Now we owe the IPC an arm and a leg.

t_ch: 我感觉金人巷也蛮繁华的？
t_en: Aurum Alley looks like it's flourishing though?

t_ch: 小吃的生意倒还算热闹，但其他生意，还有码头的运营却到了捉襟见肘的地步。
t_en: The food hawkers are thriving, but dock operations and other businesses are stretched to the breaking point.

t_ch: 金人巷码头营收要如何改善？
t_en: What can we do to fix Aurum Alley's balance sheet?

t_ch: 要是能改善就能赚钱，然后就能还上欠款了吧？
t_en: If we can make the businesses more profitable, then maybe we have a chance of paying back the IPC?

t_ch: 「开源节流」就能增加码头的营收。如今「开源」困难重重，很多金人巷商铺拒绝与货运码头合作……
t_en: If we cut costs and tap into new revenue streams, we can turn things around. A lot of vendors in Aurum Alley don't want anything to do with the dock though, so it may be hard to find new sources of revenue...

t_ch: 那「节流」呢，什么是节流？
t_en: What about cutting costs?

t_ch: 「节流」就是降低成本，如各位所见，码头也没有全部开放，而工人嘛……
t_en: Cutting costs means reducing expenses. As you can see, the dock isn't even fully utilized, and the workers, well...

t_ch: 喂喂喂，你们商会可别想着要裁人啊！
t_en: Hey, hey, hey, don't get any funny ideas about laying people off!

t_ch: 小秘书也在想办法嘛。要我说，现在发送订单的效率有些低，要是能在这花些心思，肯定能节约成本。
t_en: Miss Secretary is just helping us think of ideas. If you ask me, I think our ordering system is rather inefficient. I'm sure we could reduce our costs if we found a way to improve it.

t_ch: 要不让我来试试？
t_en: Can I give it a try?

t_ch: 没想到您还有这样的本事，请您一定得试试。
t_en: I didn't realize you were such an entrepreneur.

t_ch: 我看这也省不了多少…
t_en: I don't think that would save much...

t_ch: 总比什么都不做要好，{NICKNAME}，我看你很擅长动脑子，你试试嘛。
t_en: It's better than doing nothing. {NICKNAME}, you're smart — why don't you give it a try?

t_ch: 试试就试试。
t_en: Couldn't hurt, I guess...

t_ch: 请一定得试试。
t_en: You'd be more than welcome.

t_ch: 你从哪儿诊断出我有脑子的？
t_en: When did you convince yourself I was smart?

t_ch: 没关系的，您可以作为商会的顾问来参与此事。就算搞砸了也没关系…毕竟现在的状况已经很糟了呀，坏又能坏到哪儿去呢。
t_en: Don't worry, the situation is already bad as it is, so it's no big deal if things don't pan out. You can be on this project as our consultant.

t_ch: 试砸了，你就没有饭票了喔！
t_en: What if I mess up? You'd have to wave bye to your snacks.

t_ch: 往好处想，万一成了呢？咱们以后在金人巷的消费说不定就被他们全包了！
t_en: Think about it this way — what if you succeed? We'd have free meals forever!

t_ch: 没关系的，您可以作为商会的顾问来参与此事。就算搞砸了也没关系…毕竟现在的状况已经很糟了呀，坏又能坏到哪儿去呢。
t_en: Don't worry, the situation is already bad as it is, so it's no big deal if things don't pan out. You can be on this project as our consultant.

t_ch: 商会会长都没得法子，这位短生种小朋友真能做到吗？唉，死马当作活马医好了！
t_en: Even the president hasn't been able to solve anything — is this short-lifer really up to the job? *sigh* At this point, I guess we've got nothing to lose...

t_ch: 哈哈哈，这肯定能省下不少货运支出。
t_en: Hahaha, this will certainly save us on shipping costs.

t_ch: 可是，这样会不会不太安全？
t_en: Isn't this... a little unsafe?

t_ch: 姑娘担心的是，确认安全的工作交给我们好了。
t_en: Don't worry, our motto is "safety first."

t_ch: 还不快夸夸我？
t_en: I'm so awesome. I've done such an amazing job!

t_ch: 好，夸夸你，你的鼻子都快翘上天了。
t_en: Geez, don't get too full of yourself...

t_ch: 你们之前都没有想到吗？
t_en: So, you've never thought of this before? *shaking my head*

t_ch: 嘘！这可不能大声说出来啊。
t_en: Hey! What's gotten into you?

t_ch: 你确实有些本事，不过要我说啊，光是提高码货的效率也没有用。「鹤运物流」他们不送货，我们也只能干等着。
t_en: Impressive... but if you ask me, inventory efficiency isn't enough to save us. If Heron Express doesn't deliver the goods, then they're just gonna sit around collecting dust.

t_ch: 明白了，你们要是方便的话，能跟我去一趟「鹤运物流」的金人巷分部吗？
t_en: I see your point. If you have the time, could you accompany me to Heron Express' Aurum Alley branch office?

t_ch: 当然可以，不过要是遇到需要动脑子的事情，我肯定帮不上忙。所以……
t_en: Of course! It's just... I'm better with brawn than brains...

t_ch: 我看这金人巷，还得靠我啊。
t_en: Looks like Aurum Alley is counting on me again.

t_ch: 不小心又给自己揽了一身事。
t_en: Seems like I could make a career out of this.

t_ch: 垣风老哥——
t_en: Mr. Yuanfeng...

t_ch: 这不是小秘书吗，你身边这几位是？
t_en: Why, it's Miss Secretary. And who do we have here?

t_ch: 我是云骑素裳，这位头上长角的小姑娘我想就不用介绍了吧！而这位是……
t_en: I'm Sushang of the Cloud Knights. I think you already know the little girl with horns on her head. And this is...

t_ch: 我是{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I'm {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我是金人巷的救世主。
t_en: I'm the savior of Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 我是谁，这并不重要。
t_en: It doesn't matter who I am.

t_ch: 这时候还要保持神秘吗？
t_en: You don't need to keep a low profile anymore...

t_ch: 你不懂，万一搞砸了…
t_en: Well, if I mess things up...

t_ch: 不留下名字比较容易逃走。
t_en: It'll be easier to escape if nobody knows my name.

t_ch: 你们好…小秘书，听说你们在码头和公司的人打赌了，没还上欠款就要当面学狗叫？
t_en: Good to meet you all... Miss Secretary, I heard you made a bet with the IPC... and if you lose you have to bark like a dog?

t_ch: …这件事传得这么快吗？
t_en: ...The word got out pretty quickly, huh?

t_ch: 哎哟，公司那些化外民年纪小不懂事，小秘书你怎么还跟着一起掺和呢？
t_en: *sigh* Those outworlders from the IPC are the worst — how did you get bogged down with them?

t_ch: 跟公司打赌的，是这位女侠。
t_en: This noble Cloud Knight here is the one who made the bet with the IPC.

t_ch: 没错，金人巷的各位不必担心，是我去打赌的。
t_en: That's right, no one else in Aurum Alley needs to worry — it's all on me.

t_ch: 学狗叫的可不一定就是咱们呀。
t_en: You didn't ask what'd happen if we win...

t_ch: 对呀，万一金人巷商会还上欠款了，学狗叫的就该是他们了。
t_en: Right — if the Merchant Guild pay back what they owe, the only one barking will be that IPC stooge.

t_ch: 码头的工人们说，即便提高了码货的效率也无济于事，想问能不能让咱物流这边……
t_en: We made our inventory more efficient, but the dock workers said we still need a way to distribute the goods. I was wondering if Heron Express would be interested...

t_ch: 你听码头工人说，他们肯定说责任不在他们。要我说，这责任也不在我们。
t_en: Those dock workers don't want to take responsibility, and I'm afraid it's not our responsibility, either.

t_ch: 那…我们应该怪谁，又怪公司吗？
t_en: Then who should we blame? The IPC?

t_ch: 公司高调宣传自己将接管金人巷，并承诺会有「商铺补助」…我怀疑他们悄悄打过招呼，让商铺们别用码头物流。
t_en: The IPC said that they would handle operations at Aurum Alley and promised to give subsidies to the vendors... I suspect they secretly told all the vendors not to use the dock for logistics.

t_ch: 码头订单少，不能怪物流。
t_en: That doesn't explain why orders are so low, though.

t_ch: …确实是这个道理。
t_en: ...That's a good point.

t_ch: …公司这是给商会下套呀。
t_en: ...Sounds like the IPC set a trap for the merchant guild.

t_ch: 这都怪我们之前没有察觉他们的计谋。
t_en: *sigh* We can only blame ourselves for letting them get away with it for so long.

t_ch: 头好痒，好像要长出脑子了。
t_en: I think my brain is about to explode from all this.

t_ch: 没事，有机会我再告诉您详细情况吧。
t_en: Don't worry, I'll explain all the details to you later.

t_ch: 要是还不上租金，亏损事小，失信事大。商会如果失去了商铺的信任，终究是名存实亡…如果没有了商会，公司在金人巷这片商区也就没有竞争对手了。
t_en: If we can't pay back the rent, the financial damages will be manageable, but the reputational damage will be irreparable. If the Merchant Guild loses everyone's trust, it'll become an empty shell of its former self... and without the Merchant Guild, the IPC will have no competitors in Aurum Alley's business district.

t_ch: 公司想把这里建设成大型物流中转仓，到时小吃铺、旧货商铺都得搬离这里……
t_en: The IPC wants to turn this place into a big logistics warehouse. By then, the food hawkers and used goods stores will have to move away...

t_ch: 啊…也就是说，「金人巷」要没了吗？
t_en: Ah... So no more Aurum Alley?

t_ch: 是啊，虽然金人巷的每一寸土地都在，但属于它的味道、它的游客，还有大家对它繁荣景象的记忆都将不复存在了。
t_en: Correct. The physical space will still exist, but the Aurum Alley we all know — its fragrances, its bustling tourists — will be nothing more than a memory.

t_ch: 说说眼前的事吧——我们要怎么做？
t_en: Let's not get ahead of ourselves — what are we gonna do about the matter at hand?

t_ch: 对于物流来说，只要合理规划物流路线，就能有效降低成本。
t_en: On the logistics side, we can reduce costs by making sure the delivery routes are planned efficiently.

t_ch: 垣风老哥，你让这位{NICKNAME}试试，{F#她}{M#他}有些本事，也许能作为参考。
t_en: Mr. Yuanfeng, please allow {NICKNAME} to try {F#her}{M#his} hand at it. {F#She}{M#He} has some skills that could prove useful in this area.

t_ch: 交给我试试吧，我不敢保证。
t_en: I can't make any promises, but I'll try my best.

t_ch: 请您一定要试试。
t_en: Please give it a try.

t_ch: 不止有些本事，而是本事大了。
t_en: I'll *deliver* us a *route* to success...

t_ch: 没错没错，{F#她}{M#他}可比我头脑灵活多了。
t_en: See? {F#She's}{M#He's} a quick thinker.

t_ch: 为什么不用玉兆来计算？
t_en: Why not use a jade abacus to calculate the routes?

t_ch: 建木灾变后，玉兆的运筹功能都被限定在特殊部门的工作上。区区小巷子里的商业规划，只能靠咱们的脑瓜了。
t_en: After the Ambrosial Arbor disaster, the use of jade abaci for any kind of strategic planning was restricted to certain departments. For business planning in Aurum Alley, we can only rely on our brains.

t_ch: 而且，商会顾问的职责不仅仅是规划，还有说服和博弈…我们想要仰仗您的可不止这份规划的才能哦。
t_en: Also, the duties of a Merchant Guild consultant include planning, negotiation and strategy... We're going to be relying on you for more than just delivery routes.

t_ch: 按照你的思路来，物流应该能减少不少成本。
t_en: With these routes in place, logistics costs will be reduced significantly.

t_ch: 我果然没有看错！你们就是能帮助金人巷复兴的救星。
t_en: I knew it! You're the heroes who can help us revitalize Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 现在能告诉我们金人巷到底发生了什么吗？
t_en: Now can you tell us what really happened to Aurum Alley?

t_ch: 金人巷原本是长乐天洞天最繁华的夜市，在过去，这条以美食和商业著称的巷子里挤满了为主人领取货物的金人…不过繁华早已过去了。
t_en: ...Well, Aurum Alley was originally the busiest night market in Exalting Sanctum. In days past, this alley, famed for its food and commerce, was brimming with aurumatons collecting goods for their masters... but that prosperity has long since passed.

t_ch: 民用金人被限制，新兴的商业中心也开始冒头。金人巷就像是走在一段平缓的下坡路上——所有人都知道它江河日下，却不以为这是严重之事。
t_en: Restrictions were placed on civilian aurumatons, and new commercial centers began to spring up. It was like being on a gentle downhill slope — everyone knew things were changing, but no one thought it was serious.

t_ch: 随着罗浮仙舟暂时停泊休养，和公司的贸易往来越发频繁，商会遭遇到真正的威胁——公司高调宣布竞标金人巷的开发。他们的承诺看得见、摸得着，并且极富侵略性。
t_en: When the Luofu was moored for maintenance and trade with the IPC increased, a new threat emerged for the Merchant Guild — the IPC's announcement of a bid for the development of Aurum Alley. Their promises were visible, tangible, and extremely aggressive.

t_ch: 「平缓的下坡路」突然断裂坍塌，没有人能够再装作视而不见。商会与金人巷本身都已经岌岌可危。
t_en: When the "gentle downhill slope" suddenly became the edge of a cliff, no one could pretend to ignore it anymore. Both the Merchant Guild and Aurum Alley itself were in jeopardy.

t_ch: 能再仔细讲讲吗？
t_en: Can you fill us in on the details?

t_ch: 金人巷商区的经营合同即将到期，公司地区主管将此视为目标，并开始布局，码头债务危机也只是布局的一部分。
t_en: The contract for operating Aurum Alley's business district is about to expire, and the IPC's regional director has seen an opportunity. They orchestrated the dock debt crisis, and that's just the beginning.

t_ch: 与此同时，这个地区不同的商铺也犹豫不决——有些人将公司视为卧榻之侧的虎豹，心怀不轨；也有人将公司承诺的高额利润看作转机……
t_en: Meanwhile, vendors in Aurum Alley have expressed mixed feelings about the IPC — some see it as a crouching tiger with malicious intentions, while others see the IPC's promise of high profits as a chance to turn things around...

t_ch: 不安的情绪已经愈演愈烈——现如今人人都担心「金人巷就要没了」。商会必须做出点什么，至少要为本地商户再争取到一些利益。
t_en: But now the chill is setting in — everyone is worried that Aurum Alley is going to disappear. The Merchant Guild has to do something to help the merchants here.

t_ch: 我大概明白了。
t_en: I get the gist of it.

t_ch: 所以，我谨代表商会正式向你们发出邀请，希望你们能作为<color=#dbc291ff>「商业顾问」</color>参与到拯救金人巷的计划中。
t_en: Therefore, on behalf of the Merchant Guild, I would like to formally extend an invitation to you all to serve as <color=#dbc291ff>"business advisors"</color> in the plan to save Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 交给我吧，我能帮得上忙的。
t_en: Leave it to me. I'm sure I can be of some use.

t_ch: 这真是太好了。
t_en: Excellent.

t_ch: 请问「商务顾问」薪资丰厚吗？
t_en: How favorable is the compensation for this position?

t_ch: 虽然商会手头并不宽裕，但绝不会亏待你的。
t_en: The Merchant Guild is tight on funds, but we'll be sure to make it worth your time.

t_ch: 不好意思，我不做亏本生意。
t_en: Sorry, but I'm not a business strategist.

t_ch: …是这样的吗？请你不要马上拒绝我。
t_en: ...Is that how you feel? Well, don't turn down the offer just yet.

t_ch: 这样吧，先和身边的朋友们商量下如何？
t_en: Why don't you talk it over with everyone first?

t_ch: 我肯定是希望你能来帮忙的。
t_en: I really hope you can help out.

t_ch: 毕竟我不帮忙，你就要道歉了。
t_en: Of course. You'd be in big trouble if I don't.

t_ch: 道歉的事倒是不打紧。但我真心想帮助金人巷。
t_en: I'm not worried about that, I just want to help save Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 我要是说不来帮忙呢？
t_en: What if I refuse?

t_ch: …你也不想我在那么多人面前学小狗叫吧？而且，我真的很想帮金人巷一把。
t_en: ...You don't want me to have to bark like a dog in front of all those people, do you? Besides, I'd really like to help the people in Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 我的老家曜青仙舟，和公司有着更密切的往来。据说最初也只是公司在此投资互市。不过时间久了，事情就有了变化……
t_en: My home, the Xianzhou Yaoqing, had much closer ties with the IPC. Initially, people thought they were just engaging in a little interstellar investment... but over time, things changed.

t_ch: 渐渐地，曜青的年轻一辈开始喜欢起公司娱乐部门生产的幻戏和玩具；穿上了剪裁贴身、设计时髦的公司服饰，都快分不清他们是仙舟人还是公司职员了。
t_en: Gradually, the Yaoqing's younger generations began to enjoy the immersia produced by the IPC's Entertainment Division. Once they started wearing IPC clothing lines, you could hardly tell if they were Xianzhou locals or IPC employees.

t_ch: 还有不少人渴望在成人礼之后去往公司的世界赚钱。
t_en: After their Coming of Age Ceremonies, a lot of them aspired to join the IPC to make it big.

t_ch: 洞天里的建筑也变得面目全非了。我小时候看见的优美飞檐变得越来越少，琉璃瓦和息壤砖也消失了。一座座表面平滑，像纪念碑一样的公司大楼开始拔地而起。
t_en: The architecture across the whole delve began to change. The graceful flying eaves I saw as a child became fewer and fewer, and the glazed tiles and Fallow Earth bricks gradually disappeared. Sleek, monolithic IPC buildings started sprouting up everywhere.

t_ch: 但凡活过五百岁朝上的曜青人，比如我娘，他们都说曜青变了。可他们也说不上这一切变化究竟是奔向一个美好的未来，还是滑向被短生种左右的滑坡…他们只是惶恐不安。
t_en: Any of the Yaoqing locals older than <unbreak>500</unbreak> — like my mother — will say the Yaoqing has changed. But they're not sure whether this change is leading to a better future, or if it's allowing short-life species to dictate their way of life... It's making everyone anxious.

t_ch: 看到明曦面对公司那家伙时，我感到了同样的不安。明明活过了数倍于对方的生命，却在面对他们时茫然不知所措。
t_en: When I saw Mingxi being confronted by the IPC, I felt the same uneasiness. She's been around several life-spans longer than that stooge, but somehow she was still at a loss for how to deal with him.

t_ch: 如果对手是功夫了得的恶人，我倒是能挺身而出过上几招。但是金人巷这事仅靠拳脚功夫也没法解决，我有劲却无处使。
t_en: If our opponents were martial arts types, I could hold my own against them... but Aurum Alley's problems aren't something we can fight off with a sword.

t_ch: 我想帮助金人巷商会，想帮他们守住金人巷，拜托你了！
t_en: I want to help the Merchant Guild, I want to help everyone at Aurum Alley... please help me help them!

t_ch: 我明明只是来金人巷吃小吃的…没想到会撞上这么多事。
t_en: *sigh* I only came here for the food... I didn't expect to get tangled up in all this dock leasing stuff.

t_ch: 不过，你要是愿意出手相助的话，金人巷也许就不用消失了。
t_en: Still, if you're willing to give it a shot, maybe Aurum Alley doesn't have to disappear.

t_ch: 仔细想想，你好像什么都没做？
t_en: Wait a sec... You haven't contributed anything so far.

t_ch: 怎么会，我劝劝你，不就算我为金人巷做出了贡献吗？
t_en: That's not true, I'm doing something right now — persuading you to help us! An admirable contribution.

t_ch: 你只是担心小吃铺都要没了？
t_en: You're just worried all your favorite food stands are gonna disappear.

t_ch: 这难道还不重要吗？这就很重要…没有美食的金人巷就像…就像不装药丸的葫芦，毫无意义。
t_en: So what!? Hmph, the food's the best bit... Aurum Alley without food would be like a pill without medicine... meaningless!

t_ch: 交给我好了。
t_en: You can count on me.

t_ch: 我就知道你肯定会答应。你真是本小姐最值得信赖的好朋友！
t_en: I knew you'd help us! A truly reliable friend!

t_ch: 写作朋友…读作饭票。
t_en: By "friend" you mean "free meal ticket," right?

t_ch: 喂喂！
t_en: Hey!

t_ch: 怎么样，您能成为金人巷的「商业顾问」吗？
t_en: What do you think? Will you become a business advisor for Aurum Alley?

t_ch: 我同意了。
t_en: Yes, I'll do it.

t_ch: 太好了。
t_en: Great!

t_ch: 思考了再三，我还是想拒绝。
t_en: After much consideration, I'm still not up to it.

t_ch: …啊？你再考虑考虑嘛。
t_en: ...Huh? Go consider some more, then!

t_ch: …真是拿你没辙，我同意了。
t_en: *sigh* Fine... I'll do it.

t_ch: 真是太好了。
t_en: Glad to hear.

t_ch: …我不做顾问，但是可以帮忙。
t_en: I don't know the first thing about business or consultancy...

t_ch: 只要您愿意帮忙就行。
t_en: A willingness to help is all we need.

t_ch: 这件事我还没跟会长说过，这样吧，我们现在就去会长那边。
t_en: I still haven't discussed any of this with the president — let's go pay them a visit.

t_ch: 垣风大哥担忧地注视着金人巷，他比看上去更加心思细腻。
t_en: Yuanfeng gazes at Aurum Alley anxiously. Seems like he's actually more meticulous than he appears.

t_ch: 连烟姐姐，请你转告会长，我找到了帮手能够拯救金人巷码头…不止如此，我认为{F#她}{M#他}能帮助金人巷再次复兴。
t_en: Miss Lianyan, please tell the president that I have someone who can help us with the leasing situation at the dock... and also bring Aurum Alley back to its former glory.

t_ch: 你好，我叫{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Hi, I'm {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 你好，我就是大救星。
t_en: Hi, I'm that "someone" who can save y'all.

t_ch: 看来你这人信心满满呢。
t_en: Well, seems like you have plenty of confidence.

t_ch: 我这个人最讨厌面试了。
t_en: I hate job interviews.

t_ch: 我亲眼见过您的能力，您绝对不用担心。
t_en: I've already seen your abilities — there's nothing to be nervous about.

t_ch: 稍等一下，我问问会长的看法。
t_en: I'll go ask the president's opinion on the matter. Please wait a moment.

t_ch: 会长相信小秘书的判断，不过还是准备了考验——请将「陈机铺」纳入到物流规划之中。
t_en: Okay, the president trusts Miss Secretary's judgment, but the applicant still needs to pass a test... You must integrate Oldies Depot into the logistics plan.

t_ch: 我就给你露一手吧。
t_en: I'mma flex my skills for you real quick.

t_ch: 别抱太高的期待…
t_en: Don't get your hopes up too high...

t_ch: 连烟靠近店门，朝着店内小声地沟通了什么，没人听清她与会长的交谈。
t_en: Lianyan approaches the store's doorway and quietly conveys something to the one within. No one hears what she said to the president.

t_ch: 会长承认了{NICKNAME}的能力，同意将金人巷商会的部分管理权限开放。
t_en: The president acknowledges your expertise and agrees to confer management responsibilities accordingly.

t_ch: 至此，金人巷商会发出正式请求——请帮助商会摆脱危机，复兴金人巷的繁荣！
t_en: Aurum Alley Merchant Guild would like to issue a formal request... Please help us emerge from this crisis and revitalize Aurum Alley!

t_ch: 我一定不负期待。
t_en: I will rise to the occasion.

t_ch: 这说法也太正式了。
t_en: You make it sound so formal.

t_ch: 现在就退出还来得及吗？
t_en: Is it too late to back out?

t_ch: 你们别在意，{F#她}{M#他}只是嘴上说说而已。
t_en: Don't mind {F#her}{M#him}, {F#she}{M#he}'s talking silly again.

t_ch: 太好了，你平时有空的话，请到码头上来找我。
t_en: Now that that's settled, please come see me at the dock when you have time.

t_ch: 在我这里可以协助码头工人<color=#dbc291ff>「码放货物」</color>，以及协助鹤运物流完成<color=#dbc291ff>「物流规划」</color>。
t_en: You'll be able to help the dock workers <color=#dbc291ff>do the inventory</color> and help Heron Express with <color=#dbc291ff>logistics planning</color>.

t_ch: 我平时也会收集有关金人巷的各类情报，<color=#dbc291ff>其中包括商铺的情报，以及各种传闻。</color>解决各类事件，也能对复兴有所帮助。
t_en: I keep tabs on all the goings-on in Aurum Alley, <color=#dbc291ff>including rumors and the latest gossip.</color> If we can solve some of the problems in the community, it could help in rejuvenating Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 如果是「急人所急」之事，我也能帮忙。
t_en: If it involves helping people in need, count me in too.

t_ch: 你可以再给我讲讲吗？
t_en: Could you repeat that?

t_ch: 没问题。
t_en: Sure.

t_ch: 如果你平时有空的话，请到码头上来找我。
t_en: Just come see me at the dock when you have time.

t_ch: 在我这里可以<color=#dbc291ff>协助码头工人「码放货物」，以及协助鹤运物流完成「物流规划」。</color>
t_en: You'll be able to help the dock workers <color=#dbc291ff>do the inventory and help Heron Express with logistics planning</color>.

t_ch: 我也会收集有关金人巷的各类情报，<color=#dbc291ff>其中包括商铺的情报，以及各种传闻。</color>解决各类事件，也能对复兴有所帮助。
t_en: I keep tabs on all the goings-on in Aurum Alley, <color=#dbc291ff>including rumors and the latest gossip</color>. If we can solve some of the problems in the community, it could help in rejuvenating Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 总之，就是去找你就对了呗？
t_en: So basically I just need to go talk to you at the dock?

t_ch: 有困难尽管来找我就对了。
t_en: Anytime you need anything, come find me.

t_ch: 眼下最要紧的就是争取在还款日到来之前完成债务目标。
t_en: The important thing right now is to generate enough revenue before the payment due date.

t_ch: 那么我先离开了，你随时可以在码头上找到我。
t_en: I'll be off to the dock then. Stop by anytime.

t_ch: 总之，金人巷的事就是我的事，要是遇到什么其他困难，你记得随时叫上我。
t_en: Any problem facing Aurum Alley is also my problem. If you encounter any difficulties, don't hesitate to come find me.

t_ch: 另外…我还是问一下吧，码头应该是能还上欠款的吧？
t_en: Also... I should probably ask... Do you really think we'll be able to pay back everything owed on the dock?

t_ch: 你放心好了。
t_en: You betcha.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}真厉害！你让我想起刚到罗浮时认识的一个好姐妹，她也和你一样既有救人所急的热心肠，一样的心灵手巧，本事不小。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you're the best! You remind me of the friend I made when I first arrived on the Luofu — she had the same eagerness for saving people, and the same tenacity for solving problems.

t_ch: 我可没法保证…
t_en: I can't make any guarantees...

t_ch: …我了解。{NICKNAME}肯出手帮助这儿，已经让我很感动了！
t_en: ...I understand, {NICKNAME}. But even then, it's impressive that you are willing to step up and help!

t_ch: 我还有云骑的任务在身，得先走一步了。得空的话，我会来金人巷帮忙的。
t_en: I have a mission from the Cloud Knights, so I have to leave for now. If I have time, I'll definitely come back to Aurum Alley to help out.

t_ch: 明明是拯救金人巷这么要紧的事，会长却根本没有露面，也不知道是怎么想的。
t_en: So strange — saving Aurum Alley is a critical issue, but the president didn't even make an appearance...

t_ch: 我们还没取得会长的信任吧。
t_en: Perhaps we haven't won their trust yet.

t_ch: 你可不能做经营码头的工具人呀。
t_en: Then why would they put you in charge of things at the dock...?

t_ch: 会长可能有什么难言之隐？
t_en: Perhaps they weren't feeling too well.

t_ch: 要是身有贵恙，我可以给帮忙看看，再开点儿药嘛。
t_en: If that's the case, I can give them a check-up and prescribe some medicine.

t_ch: 一不小心在这儿逗留了太久了，{NICKNAME}，我要先回丹鼎司瞧瞧，免得他们担心。你就好好加油吧！
t_en: Wow, time sure does fly when you're trying to save the world. {NICKNAME}, I need to get back to the Alchemy Commission. Keep up the good work around here!

t_ch: 我知道你想问问有关会长的消息，不过，我建议你眼下专注于码头的事务。
t_en: I know you want to ask about the president, but I suggest you focus your energy on managing the dock for now.

t_ch: 会长一直在关心各位的进展，不必担心。
t_en: Don't worry, the president is paying very close attention to our progress in Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 既然你来了，我们就开始处理码头相关的工作吧。
t_en: Now that you're here, let's get started on the dock-related work.

t_ch: 来得正好，大家正在向公司这家伙讨说法呢！
t_en: You're just in time — everyone is asking this IPC goon for an explanation!

t_ch: 你张嘴就是「公司这家伙」，也太不懂礼貌了吧。
t_en: Calling someone an "IPC goon" isn't very becoming of a Cloud Knight now, is it?

t_ch: 今天我不代表公司，我只是个金人巷的游客，你可以叫我斯科特。
t_en: I'm not here today as a representative of the IPC, I'm just a tourist. You can just call me Skott.

t_ch: 你好，斯科特。
t_en: Hello, Skott.

t_ch: 你看，这位{F#女士}{M#先生}就比你通情达理多了。
t_en: You see, this {F#young lady}{M#gentleman} understands etiquette.

t_ch: 你好，公司这家伙。
t_en: Hello, IPC goon.

t_ch: 我也不和你一般计较。
t_en: ...I have no quarrel with you.

t_ch: 我说，我只是寄个快递，怎么被你们围起来了？
t_en: Hmph, can't a man come here to deliver a package without being surrounded?

t_ch: 这家伙的货物有问题，会损坏其他客人的商品，害得码头一直在赔偿……
t_en: There's something wrong with the stuff this guy is shipping — it's causing damage to other people's goods, and our dock here is liable for the compensation...

t_ch: 我们要检查你的快递包裹。
t_en: We want to check your courier package.

t_ch: 你要不要自己解释一下原因？
t_en: Would you like to explain yourself?

t_ch: 哈——？说大声点，我听不太清。你们金人巷就是这么对待游客的？
t_en: Ha...? Speak louder, I can't quite hear you. Is this how you treat guests in Aurum Alley?

t_ch: 我们现在就去打开他的快递，肯定有鬼。
t_en: Let's open that package right here, right now — then we'll see what he's hiding!

t_ch: 你可得想清楚了，要是我的东西坏掉了，你赔得起吗？
t_en: I'd be careful if I were you — if you damage my goods, you'll have to pay for them.

t_ch: 如果是你的快递有问题呢？
t_en: What if we discover a serious issue with your package?

t_ch: 哈——？你说我的快递有问题，我还说你们有问题呢。
t_en: Ha...? A serious issue? I think you'll be the ones with the serious issue!

t_ch: 我们赔得起，现在就拆。
t_en: We can compensate you for any damages. Open it.

t_ch: 哈——？我没听错吧，{F#她}{M#他}说{F#她}{M#他}赔得起，真是让人意外。
t_en: Ha...? Did I hear that right? {F#She}{M#He} said {F#She}{M#he} can afford to pay for it. What a surprise.

t_ch: 少在那里阴阳怪气，我们现在就拆。
t_en: No need to make this difficult. Let's open it.

t_ch: 素裳和工人将斯科特的快递摆在码头的空地上。
t_en: Sushang and workers place Skott's package on the floor.

t_ch: 小秘书有些紧张，她也不知道如何是好。
t_en: Mingxi is a bit nervous. She's not sure how to proceed.

t_ch: 去检查检查货物里有什么蹊跷吧。
t_en: Let's check and see if there's anything suspicious.

t_ch: 精致的陶瓷花瓶。年代久远，体态均匀，瓶身上自然形成的纹理独一无二。这些瓷器大多昂贵并且容易碎裂，它不应该出现在这样的小小货运港里。
t_en: Intricate porcelain vase that can be dated to the distant past. Its well-balanced proportion and the naturally-formed patterns on its body make it stand out from the rest of its kind. Such an expensive and brittle vase shouldn't have been sent here.

t_ch: 漂亮的博古架。色泽沉穆，线条素雅，造型古朴，整体虚实相宜，空灵有致。文化人喜欢将笔墨纸砚、瓶瓶罐罐放在架子上，获得一番仙逸的快乐。
t_en: A beautiful antiques display shelf with sober color and an elegant contour. Its classic style lends itself an air of serenity. The shelf provides a suitable home for brushes and inkstones and pulls you away from the mundane hustle.

t_ch: 被叫作「浓云金蟾」的香炉。虽然迄今为止，你已经与太多的入魔机巧战斗过，你很难得见到如此安静的家伙。
t_en: A crucible named "Golden Cloud Toad." Considering how many hostile ingenia you've encountered, this one is suspiciously quiet.

t_ch: 等等，你的经验告诉你，它既然出现在这里，少不了会引来一场恶斗。不出意外，眼前这只「浓云金蟾」适时醒了过来。
t_en: Wait, you sense a malicious intent brewing in the air. A fight is imminent. As expected, the toad springs to life.

t_ch: 它算不上是你的威胁，但是旁边的易碎品可就不这么想了……
t_en: It's not that much of a threat for you, but the silent screaming of the fragile goods nearby is too loud to be ignored.

t_ch: 你对这台机巧做了手脚，货船上的其他货物就是被它损坏的。
t_en: You tampered with this ingenium so that it caused damage to other goods on the freight skiff.

t_ch: 哈——？说大声点，我听不太清。
t_en: Ha...? Speak louder, I can't quite hear you.

t_ch: 现在证据确凿，你还要抵赖？
t_en: You still deny it when faced with conclusive evidence?

t_ch: 证据确凿？抵赖？
t_en: Conclusive evidence? Deny?

t_ch: 你们看仔细了，这个什么古董怪物根本就不是我的。
t_en: Here, look carefully — this old piece of junk doesn't even belong to me.

t_ch: 什么？！
t_en: What!?

t_ch: 不信，你们可以问码头工人。
t_en: If you don't believe me, just ask the dock workers.

t_ch: 是、是这样的，这台「浓云金蟾」不是斯科特先生的。
t_en: I—If I may, h—he's telling the truth. This Golden Cloud Toad does not belong to Mr. Skott.

t_ch: 那为什么这个什么蛤蟆会摆在这里？
t_en: Then why was the toad here in the first place?

t_ch: 那是你们自己的问题，不关我的事，对吧？
t_en: That's your problem, not mine!

t_ch: 我反倒要问问，我的花瓶本来包装得好好的，差点就要被砸碎了，这算怎么回事？
t_en: Now let me ask you a question — my vase was almost smashed to pieces — what do you say about that?

t_ch: 这不是还没有碎吗？
t_en: "Almost" being the operative word...

t_ch: 哦，你还希望它碎啊？
t_en: Oh? You wanted it to be broken?

t_ch: 检查完毕，准备发货。
t_en: The inspection is over. We'll send the goods on their way.

t_ch: 哈——？我没听错吧。
t_en: Ha...? Did I hear that right?

t_ch: 你们闹了这一出，不给我道个歉，可就说不过去了。
t_en: After making a big scene like that, don't you owe me an apology?

t_ch: 这…这还要道歉。
t_en: I... I guess we do.

t_ch: 哈——？这要是不道歉，传出去可不太好听呀。
t_en: Well...? Are you going to apologize or not?

t_ch: …对、对不起。
t_en: ...Sorry, sir.

t_ch: 无所谓，你就当是提前练习吧。道歉这种事，一次可不够呀，哈哈哈哈。
t_en: For this kind of barbarity, a single apology won't ever be enough. Just think of this as a practice round for later, hahahaha.

t_ch: 唔嗯嗯嗯嗯…
t_en: Grrrr...

t_ch: 这要是我，我就不会道歉。
t_en: If it were me, I wouldn't be apologizing for anything.

t_ch: 这要是你，应该就不会被算计了。
t_en: If it were you, you wouldn't have fallen for his trap.

t_ch: 我们被算计了，没有办法。
t_en: He fooled us. That's all there is to it.

t_ch: 就让他得逞吧，下次就不会了。
t_en: He fooled us this once, but he won't fool us next time.

t_ch: 唔嗯嗯嗯嗯…
t_en: Grrrrrrrr...

t_ch: 唔嗯嗯嗯嗯…
t_en: Grrrrrrrrrrrr...

t_ch: 抱歉，我根本就没有搭上话。
t_en: Sorry, I should've stepped in.

t_ch: 我娘告诉我，只要我练就了一身本事，别人就能与我讲道理了…看来我这本事还不够。
t_en: My mother always told me that as long as I honed my skills, I'd be able to deal with any situation... Seems I've still got honing to do.

t_ch: 在还款日到来之前，我得让二位学会吵架、不对…学会辩论才行！走吧，我们去找会长。
t_en: Before the payment due date arrives, I'm going to teach you two how to fight — with words! Follow me — we're going to see the president.

t_ch: 小秘书已经解释过来意，你们是想要学会在与人争论时占据上风，对吗？
t_en: Miss Secretary said that you want to learn how to get the upper hand in an argument. Is that right?

t_ch: 连烟比小秘书更像是小秘书。
t_en: Lianyan, you seem more like a secretary than Miss Secretary.

t_ch: 我只是个传声筒，可不是什么秘书。
t_en: I'm no secretary — more of a messenger.

t_ch: 怎么还见不到会长啊？急死了。
t_en: When are we gonna get to see the president?

t_ch: 会长说过以后会和各位见面的，现在还不是时候。
t_en: The president agreed to meet with you all later, but now is not the time.

t_ch: 会长知道码头上最近发生的事，愿意将简单的谈判技巧传授给各位。
t_en: The president is aware of recent events and is willing to pass on some simple negotiation skills to you.

t_ch: 第一条，<color=#dbc291ff>「提前准备好底牌」</color>。就像云骑军上战场之前要备好武器，走上谈判桌之前，你也得确认好自己的底牌。
t_en: Rule one: <color=#dbc291ff>Prepare your ace card in advance</color>. Just as Cloud Knights ready their weapons before entering battle, you should have your ace card ready before you walk up to the negotiating table.

t_ch: 我现在就给你们几个「材料」，你们一定要仔细确认。
t_en: I will present you with a few items, and you need to examine them carefully.

t_ch: 快递箱？
t_en: A courier package?

t_ch: 这是码头闹剧中装着「浓云金蟾」的快递箱。发件人处被刻意划掉了姓名、联系方式，鹤运物流记录中显示寄件人是阿丰。
t_en: This is the box that was opened earlier at the dock — the one containing the Golden Cloud Toad. The name and contact information of the sender were deliberately crossed out, but records from the Heron Express show that the sender is A-Feng.

t_ch: 收支记录？
t_en: Income and expenditure records?

t_ch: 这是码头工人阿丰的收支记录，近日收到了公司分多次汇入的信用点。
t_en: The records show that a dock worker named A-Feng recently received credit remitted by the IPC, spaced out over several installments.

t_ch: 这种记录是怎么搞到的？
t_en: How did you obtain all these?

t_ch: 会长自有手段。
t_en: The president has their ways.

t_ch: 这些收入是有些可疑。
t_en: Suspicious...

t_ch: 材料我都确认过一遍了。
t_en: I've looked over everything.

t_ch: 第二条，<color=#dbc291ff>「在合适的时机出牌」</color>。只有当对方话语中露出破绽时，这些「材料」才能发挥真正的作用。
t_en: Rule Two: <color=#dbc291ff>Play your ace card at the right time</color>. These documents can only be truly effective when your opponent reveals a weak spot.

t_ch: 这样吧，我为你们准备了一次练习，作为指导的结束。过来吧，阿丰师傅。
t_en: How about this — I've prepared a little practice exercise for you. Mr. A-Feng, over here please.

t_ch: 这会长到底有些什么本事啊……
t_en: What exactly does the president have up their sleeve...

t_ch: 我没有什么可说的，我什么都不知道。
t_en: I don't know anything. I don't have anything to say.

t_ch: 我叫阿丰，是我将那个带有「浓云金蟾」的箱子带出来的。
t_en: My name's A-Feng — I was the one who brought out the box containing the Golden Cloud Toad.

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 我没有看到什么箱子，只看到了「浓云金蟾」。
t_en: I didn't see any boxes, only the Golden Cloud Toad.

t_ch: 你们要检查货物，我们当然就把货箱拆了，不然，你要自己拆吗？
t_en: If goods need to be inspected, we remove the packaging ahead of time — you wouldn't want to do all that work yourself, would you?

t_ch: 别人叫我搬，我就搬了，我也没想到里面装着故障机巧，这件事和我没有关系。
t_en: I was told to move something, and I did. I had no knowledge of the presence of an ingenium. This matter has nothing to do with me.

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 你为什么强调这事和自己没有关系？
t_en: Why do you emphasize that this matter has nothing to do with you?

t_ch: 我不是怕你误会嘛，就好像是我故意放进去的一样。
t_en: I just want to avoid any misunderstanding, in case someone has it in their head that I intentionally misplaced an item...

t_ch: （这句话好像不太对劲……）
t_en: (I sense a hole in his logic...)

t_ch: 出示
t_en: (Present evidence)

t_ch: 你不可能和这件事没关系。
t_en: I think this has a lot to do with you...

t_ch: 鹤运物流上有记录显示，这箱快递就是「阿丰」寄出去的，你怎么说？
t_en: Records from Heron Express show that this container was sent by a person named "A-Feng." What do you have to say about that?

t_ch: 啊，这个，会不会是哪里搞错了呢？
t_en: Ah! Th—That... must be some kind of mistake.

t_ch: 我也是码头工人，我怎么可能向着星际和平公司呢……
t_en: I'm a dock worker, after all. How could I conspire with the IPC?

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 码头工人就必须和星际和平公司对立吗？
t_en: Does being a dock worker mean that person can't conspire with the IPC?

t_ch: 谁不知道他们想要利用码头债务，让商会彻底失去信誉嘛。商会雇佣了我，又不是公司雇佣了我，我不可能支持公司的。
t_en: Everyone knows the IPC wants to use the dock debt to discredit the Merchant Guild. The Merchant Guild are the ones who gave me a job, not the IPC — why would I side with them?

t_ch: （这句话好像不太对劲……）
t_en: (I sense a hole in his logic...)

t_ch: 出示
t_en: (Present evidence)

t_ch: 你看看这个。
t_en: Take a look at this.

t_ch: 你收到了公司的信用点，为了这点好处，你帮助公司破坏码头的运输活动。
t_en: You received payments from the IPC, and in exchange you sabotaged the shipping operations at the dock.

t_ch: 出示。
t_en: (Present evidence)

t_ch: 你看看这个。
t_en: Take a look at this.

t_ch: 这和现在的话题无关吧。
t_en: This has nothing to do with the topic at hand.

t_ch: 我叫阿丰，是我将那个带有「浓云金蟾」的箱子带出来的。
t_en: My name's A-Feng — I was the one who brought out the box containing the Golden Cloud Toad.

t_ch: 别人叫我搬，我就搬了，我也没想到里面装着故障机巧，这件事和我没有关系。
t_en: I was told to move something, and I did. I had no knowledge of the presence of an ingenium. This matter has nothing to do with me.

t_ch: 我也是码头工人，我怎么可能向着星际和平公司呢……
t_en: I'm a dock worker, after all. How could I conspire with the IPC?

t_ch: 唠唠叨叨，别再问了！
t_en: Alright, alright, enough already!

t_ch: 公司担心码头会还清债务，就想让我做些小动作…可那又咋样？
t_en: The IPC was worried that the lease for the dock would be paid off, so they had me move some things around... is that so terrible?

t_ch: 这金人巷交给公司管理有什么不好的。你们这些迂腐的家伙别耽误大家挣钱啊！
t_en: Is letting the IPC take over management of Aurum Alley such a bad thing? Besides, what's it to you if I make some extra money?

t_ch: 阿丰先生…商会的目标是促进金人巷复兴，怎么会是耽误大家呢？
t_en: Mr. A-Feng... Our goal at the Merchant Guild is to help make Aurum Alley a better place for everyone. Why would you work against that?

t_ch: 你就继续自欺欺人吧，这金人巷早晚得变成公司的大仓库！
t_en: Keep your heads in the sand if you like, but it's only a matter of time before Aurum Alley becomes another warehouse for the IPC!

t_ch: 你应该堂堂正正地反对。
t_en: Be a man! Do the right thing!

t_ch: 没错，背地里做小动作可不行。
t_en: That's right! You shouldn't be betraying your fellow workers for a quick profit.

t_ch: 码头现在可是蒸蒸日上。
t_en: But the business on the dock is flourishing.

t_ch: 就算能还上码头的租金，那用来竞标金人巷的资金呢，你们有吗？
t_en: Hmph, even if you pay back the debt, where are you gonna get enough money to win the bid for Aurum Alley?

t_ch: 其实我也支持公司的做法。
t_en: Well, actually, I side with the IPC as well.

t_ch: 你当我傻吗，我不信。
t_en: Hmph, what do you take me for? I'm not falling for that.

t_ch: 看来真相已经水落石出了。请收下阿丰师傅的证词，你们也许能用得上。
t_en: It seems that the truth has come out. Here is Mr. A-Feng's testimony — it may come in useful later.

t_ch: 码头的租金也已经备妥了，你要是准备好接下来的谈判，就来码头上找我吧。
t_en: The payment for the dock is ready now. If you're ready for the next round of negotiations, come and see me at the dock.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，新账旧账一起算，我得让那家伙当众道歉！
t_en: Right, that guy's gonna be sorry for everything he's done. Time we got a public apology!

t_ch: 公司的业务员代表已经到了，我也叫上了天舶司的夕葵小姐公证。
t_en: The IPC representative has arrived, and I've called for Miss Xikui from the Sky-Faring Commission to act as a notary.

t_ch: 我准备好了。
t_en: I'm ready.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，我只有拳头能说过他，嘴上的事情就靠你了。
t_en: Remember, this is a war of words... over to you.

t_ch: 我还没有准备好。
t_en: I'm not ready yet.

t_ch: 在正式交涉前，我们要确认一下手头的「材料」。
t_en: Before we start, let's review the documents we have.

t_ch: 金人巷码头租金？
t_en: The payment for the dock?

t_ch: 我们在规定的还款日内完成了目标金额。这是我们能够继续谈判的基础，也是金人巷商业活力的证明。
t_en: We reached the required amount by the repayment date. This is the basis for our right to continue negotiations and is a testament to the business viability of Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 码头租赁合同？
t_en: The leasing contract for the dock?

t_ch: 金人巷码头公开招标后，公司凭借「外贸资源」和「雄厚资本」成功中标。随后，公司将码头租赁给金人巷商会实际运营。
t_en: When the Aurum Alley dock went up for public bid, the IPC won the bid using its interstellar trade resources and extensive capital. Then the IPC leased the dock out to the Aurum Alley Merchant Guild for management and operations.

t_ch: 这份合同能够证明金人巷商会经营码头的正当权利。
t_en: This contract can prove that the Merchant Guild has legitimate rights to operate the dock.

t_ch: 码头工人的证言？
t_en: The dock worker's testimony?

t_ch: 码头工人阿丰的证言，他承认自己接受公司的好处，并破坏了码头物流的正常运作。
t_en: The testimony of dock worker A-Feng, who has admitted to disrupting the normal operations of the dock in exchange for payment from the IPC.

t_ch: 材料我都确认过一遍了。
t_en: I've looked over everything.

t_ch: 走吧，天舶司的公证人已经在那边等我们了。
t_en: Let's go. The notary from the Sky-Faring Commission is already waiting for us.

t_ch: 我谨代表天舶司，协调星际和平公司与金人巷商会之间的租金纠纷。
t_en: On behalf of the Sky-Faring Commission, I am here to preside over the leasing dispute between the IPC and Aurum Alley Merchant Guild.

t_ch: 这还需要什么协调吗？我就直说吧——商会早就该滚出金人巷了。
t_en: Is there anything left to dispute? I'll cut to the chase — the Merchant Guild should have removed itself from Aurum Alley a long time ago.

t_ch: 公司就不能派个正常人过来谈判吗？
t_en: Couldn't the IPC choose a sane person to represent them?

t_ch: 哈——？你说大声点，我听不太清。
t_en: Ha...? Say that a little louder, I can't quite hear you.

t_ch: <size=28>我说，公司是、没、有、别、人、了、吗！</size>
t_en: <size=28>I said: The — IPC — should — send — someone — else!</size>

t_ch: 呜…这小妞的嗓门好惊人，我耳膜都要穿孔了……
t_en: Goodness! There's no need to shout, little miss feisty — my ears are ringing!

t_ch: 呼，希望待会儿你当众向我道歉的时候，也要像刚才那样大声！
t_en: Hmph, you should be saving your voice for your public apology...

t_ch: 我作为公司的商业代表，出席这场谈判合法、合情、合理！你们金人巷商会打算派谁上阵呀？路过的云骑军？躲的不见踪影的懦夫会长？……
t_en: As the representative of the IPC, my presence in this negotiation is legal, reasonable and sensible! Who do you plan on sending to the battlefield? A random Cloud Knight? That cowardly president who's afraid to even show their face?

t_ch: 还是这个临时被你们拉来，一脸别人欠了{F#她}{M#他}五十信用点的{F#小妞}{M#小子}？
t_en: Or how about that kid who got dragged into this fiasco at the last minute, walking around like {F#she}{M#he} owes someone <unbreak>50</unbreak> credits?

t_ch: …够了，斯科特代表，请阐述公司方面的诉求。
t_en: ...That's enough, Representative Skott. Please state the IPC's side of the case.

t_ch: 金人巷商会，请准备好发言。
t_en: Aurum Alley Merchant Guild, please prepare to make your opening statements.

t_ch: 公司特地将经营权租让给金人巷商会，就是为了谋求共同发展。
t_en: The IPC specially leased operations of the dock to Aurum Alley Merchant Guild in the hopes of seeking common interest and development.

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 公司为什么不能自己运营码头？
t_en: Why couldn't the IPC operate the dock from the beginning?

t_ch: 哦，你是想说公司是在给金人巷商会下套吗？
t_en: Oh, are you implying that the IPC was setting a trap for the Merchant Guild?

t_ch: 公司将码头租给商会，然后商会经营不善，以便彻底破坏商会积累的信誉？
t_en: You're saying the IPC leased the dock to the Merchant Guild — who mismanaged it — in order to destroy the Merchant Guild's credibility?

t_ch: 你们肯定就是这样想的。
t_en: That was definitely your plan all along.

t_ch: 你这就是在承认商会肯定经营不好码头。
t_en: So you admit that the Merchant Guild mismanaged the dock.

t_ch: 呃呃呃…可恶的家伙……
t_en: Ughhhh... this guy...

t_ch: 但是商会拒绝与金人巷的各大商铺进行合作，曾经繁华一时的码头如今门可罗雀。
t_en: But the Merchant Guild refused to cooperate with the major shops in Aurum Alley, and the once prosperous dock is now desolate.

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 商会从来没有拒绝和金人巷商铺合作。
t_en: The Merchant Guild has never refused to work with any shops in Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 没有拒绝？那我问问你们，现在有几家商铺加入到你们的物流网络里了？
t_en: Never refused? Then let me ask you — how many shops are currently in your logistics network?

t_ch: 以后会多起来的。
t_en: There will be a lot more later.

t_ch: 以后？多久以后？按你们仙舟人的时间观念，五百年以后？哈哈，这里的商铺们等得起吗？
t_en: Later? How much later? For the Xianzhou, does "later" mean <unbreak>500</unbreak> years? Haha, can the shop owners here wait that long?

t_ch: 呃…这…十年？二十年？{NICKNAME}，怎么办，我、我也说不上来。
t_en: Uh... ten years? Twenty years? Psst, {NICKNAME}, what should I say? Is twenty about right?

t_ch: 金人巷码头运输效率低下、成交量低，根本没有创造价值，这就是经营不善的证据。
t_en: The inefficient shipping and low volume of transactions at the Aurum Alley dock — which does not create any value at all — are clear evidence of poor management.

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 码头已经改善了运输效率，发货量也在不断增加。
t_en: The dock has improved shipping efficiency and transaction volume is increasing.

t_ch: 哈——？你们不会以为这点交易量就能满足了吧？
t_en: Ha...? You guys didn't think that this transaction volume would be enough, did you?

t_ch: 金人巷的商铺们可不是来陪你们过家家的，大家都是要挣钱的。
t_en: The shopkeepers in Aurum Alley aren't here to play house with you — we're all trying to turn a profit.

t_ch: 再加上商会无力支付租金，我方认为商会应该主动放弃码头经营权，并支付额外赔偿。
t_en: Coupled with the Merchant Guild's inability to pay the rent, we believe the Guild should voluntarily relinquish operating rights to the dock and pay additional compensation.

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 你凭什么认为我们无力支付租金？
t_en: What makes you think we can't afford to pay the lease?

t_ch: 你们要能还上，<color=#dbc291ff>就拿出证据来呀</color>。
t_en: If you have the necessary funds, <color=#dbc291ff>prove it</color>.

t_ch: （我们可以用什么证明？）
t_en: (Do we have any proof?)

t_ch: 出示
t_en: (Present evidence)

t_ch: 码头已经还上了。
t_en: We've already paid back the lease.

t_ch: 这是金人巷商会的租金。商会已经扭亏为盈，能够赔付欠款了。
t_en: These are the funds from Aurum Alley Merchant Guild. The Guild has turned a profit and is able to pay back what it owes.

t_ch: 什、什么？！你们居然还上了？凭什么！
t_en: Wh—What? You already paid back the lease? How!?

t_ch: 金人巷商会提供的材料真实可信，根据双方的租赁合同来看，商会依然持有码头经营权。
t_en: The documentation provided by Aurum Alley Merchant Guild is true and credible, and according to the lease contract between the two parties, the Merchant Guild still holds the rights to operate the dock.

t_ch: 接下来，商会一方提出，斯科特代表一直在暗中破坏码头经营活动，对此你有什么要解释的吗？
t_en: Next order of business — the Merchant Guild has accused Representative Skott of secretly sabotaging dock operations. Do you have anything to say about that?

t_ch: 你们少血口喷人了，我为什么要搞小动作？你们这破码头，既没有商铺合作，又频繁出现事故，这跟我有什么关系？
t_en: Don't be so slanderous! Why would I stoop so low? What do the frequent accidents at this dock have to do with me?

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 码头上出运输事故，你怎么会知道呢？
t_en: How did you know about the frequent accidents at the dock?

t_ch: 我也用码头物流的嘛，我寄出去的快递总是在路上被损坏，你们有什么头绪吗？
t_en: I use the shipping services too, you know — do you have any idea how often my packages are damaged in transit?

t_ch: 你少在那里装糊涂了，这不是都怪你吗？
t_en: Don't feign ignorance — isn't that all by your own design?

t_ch: 再说了，我又不参与码头发货，难不成还能是我买通了码头工人吗？真是好笑。
t_en: Besides, I'm not even involved in shipping operations — how could I possibly buy off a dock worker? The absurdity!

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 有没有一种可能，你真的买通了码头工人呢？
t_en: Is it possible that you paid off a dock worker to do your bidding?

t_ch: 空口无凭，<color=#dbc291ff>拿出证据来呀</color>。
t_en: Empty rhetoric — <color=#dbc291ff>let's see some evidence</color>.

t_ch: （我们可以用什么证明？）
t_en: (Do we have any proof?)

t_ch: 出示
t_en: (Present evidence)

t_ch: 阿丰已经全部交代了。
t_en: We have the testimony of A-Feng.

t_ch: 这份证言铁证如山，就是你指使他破坏客人商品的。
t_en: This testimony is concrete proof that you instructed him to sabotage shipments.

t_ch: 什、什么？！那个老古董…仙舟人不是一直宣扬什么讲义气吗？我给他那么多钱，他转眼就把我卖了！可恶啊——
t_en: Wh—What? That little... Aren't the Xianzhou people always preaching about righteousness? I gave him all that money, and he sold me out in a heartbeat! Darn it...

t_ch: 出示。
t_en: (Present evidence)

t_ch: 你看看这个。
t_en: Take a look at this.

t_ch: 哈——？你说这个谁懂啊，快拿回去吧。
t_en: Ha...? What's this about? Get that out of here.

t_ch: 公司特地将经营权租让给金人巷商会，就是为了谋求共同发展。
t_en: The IPC specially leased operations of the dock to Aurum Alley Merchant Guild in the hopes of seeking common interest and development.

t_ch: 但是商会拒绝与金人巷的各大商铺进行合作，曾经繁华一时的码头如今门可罗雀。
t_en: The Merchant Guild refused to cooperate with the major shops in Aurum Alley, and the once prosperous dock is now desolate.

t_ch: 金人巷码头运输效率低下、成交量低，根本没有创造价值，这就是经营不善的证据。
t_en: The inefficient shipping and low volume of transactions at the Aurum Alley dock — which does not create any value at all — are clear evidence of poor management.

t_ch: 再加上商会无力支付租金，我方认为商会应该主动放弃码头经营权，并支付额外赔偿。
t_en: Coupled with the Merchant Guild's inability to pay the rent, we believe the Guild should voluntarily relinquish operating rights to the dock and pay additional compensation.

t_ch: 你们少血口喷人了，我为什么要搞小动作？你们这破码头，既没有商铺合作，又频繁出现事故，这跟我有什么关系？
t_en: Don't be so slanderous! Why would I stoop so low? What do the frequent accidents at this dock have to do with me?

t_ch: 再说了，我又不参与码头发货，难不成还能是我买通了码头工人吗？真是好笑。
t_en: Bah! I'm not even involved in shipping operations — how could I possibly buy off a dock worker? The absurdity!

t_ch: 如果你们提供的证据属实，斯科特先生，这是不正当的商业行为，天舶司会继续调查。
t_en: If the evidence presented is true, Mr. Skott, then this constitutes improper business practices and the Sky-Faring Commission will conduct a follow-up investigation.

t_ch: 怎么会这样，到底是哪一步出了岔子……
t_en: How did this happen? How did the plan go awry...?

t_ch: 别忘了，我们之前的赌约，你要当着大家的面向我道歉！
t_en: Don't forget about our bet! You have to apologize in front of everyone!

t_ch: 我不！凭什么要我向你道歉！
t_en: What? Why should I apologize!?

t_ch: 就凭你践踏了他人的努力，就凭你使用了肮脏的手段！
t_en: Because you used dirty tricks to trample all over the honest work of others!

t_ch: 如果我道歉，就能让热爱金人巷的人们不必心寒意冷的话，我会道歉一百次一千次！
t_en: An apology alone won't be enough to fix the damage. If it were — I'd make you do it a thousand times over!

t_ch: 但现在该道歉的人是你！
t_en: Consider yourself lucky it's just the once!

t_ch: 我、我对不起金人巷……
t_en: I... hereby apologize to Aurum Alley...

t_ch: 哈——？我听不见，大声点。
t_en: Ha...? Speak louder, I can't quite hear you.

t_ch: 还有呢，你当时说的那句话呢？
t_en: Don't forget the other part of the bet.

t_ch: 素裳，你还挺会讲啊！
t_en: Sushang is kicking your butt, dude.

t_ch: 真的吗？我只是有感而发而已。
t_en: Really? I was just speaking from the heart.

t_ch: 快点！
t_en: Hurry up!

t_ch: 我、我要向金人巷的父老乡亲们道歉——
t_en: I—I apologize to all the people of Aurum Alley...

t_ch: 「我是个有眼无珠的蠢货！不知天高地厚，只会狺狺狂吠！」
t_en: I'm a raving idiot howling into the wind!

t_ch: 你还没学狗叫呢？
t_en: I don't hear barking...

t_ch: …真的要这样做吗？
t_en: ...Is that really necessary?

t_ch: （适当地沉默）
t_en: (Appropriate silence)

t_ch: 你当时怎么说的，学狗叫呢？
t_en: Wasn't there something about barking like a dog?

t_ch: 对啊，对啊。
t_en: Make him bark, make him bark!

t_ch: 啊——够了！
t_en: Ugh — enough!

t_ch: 汪汪！汪汪汪汪汪！汪——
t_en: Woof, woof! Woof, woof, woof, woof, woof! Woof!

t_ch: 汪汪嗷嗷呜——
t_en: Woof woof owwww—

t_ch: 啧，真是丑态百出，今天就到此为止吧。
t_en: Tsk, what a clownshow. This arbitration is hereby resolved.

t_ch: 斯科特代表垂头丧气地离开码头。
t_en: Skott leaves the dock with his head hanging low.

t_ch: 码头的债务危机暂告一段落，金人巷的复兴才刚刚开始。
t_en: The dock's crisis is over for now. The revival of Aurum Alley is just beginning.

t_ch: 多亏了各位，我们成功在码头的债务危机中顺利通过。
t_en: Thanks to all of you, we managed to pull through the dock's debt crisis.

t_ch: 哎，这得多亏了我呀。
t_en: Yes, especially thanks to me.

t_ch: 这毫无疑问是你们的功劳。
t_en: Undoubtedly, yes.

t_ch: 这个过程，实在是有些艰辛。
t_en: It was a tough journey.

t_ch: 但是这一切都是值得的。
t_en: Worth every step.

t_ch: 现在应该趁着好势头，将金人巷码头全面升级，进一步提升码头的交易量。
t_en: We should take advantage of the momentum to upgrade the Aurum Alley dock and increase its transaction volume.

t_ch: 我筹备着将<color=#dbc291ff>「码放货物」和「物流规划」进行升级</color>，之前闲置的码头，现在也准备开放使用了，你要是有空就来找我确认吧。
t_en: I'm preparing to <color=#dbc291ff>upgrade inventory and logistics planning</color>. The previously-idle dock is now ready for use, so come see me when you have time.

t_ch: 码头危机暂时结束，但是「金人巷复兴」才刚刚开始呢！
t_en: The dock crisis is over for now, but the rejuvenation of Aurum Alley has just begun!

t_ch: 多亏了各位的努力，金人巷不仅还上了码头的租金债务，还提升了码头的吞吐量，我安排码头工人将原本闲置的码头清理了出来，升级了「码放货物」的规模。
t_en: Thanks to your help, the lease was paid off and the dock capacity is increasing. I told the workers to clear out areas that weren't utilized and use them to store extra cargo.

t_ch: 这下码头能够支持跟更多的商家合作了，让我们一起为金人巷复兴努力吧！
t_en: Now the dock can support collaboration with more businesses. Let's keep working together for the revival of Aurum Alley!

t_ch: 正好有一件事要通知你——我们顺利地完成了这一阶段的营收目标！这都是多亏了你、素裳、还有金人巷各位商户们的帮助。
t_en: I have something to tell you — we achieved our revenue goal for this quarter! This is all thanks to you two, not to mention the merchants of Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 商会承诺，我们不会辜负大家的期待，会将这笔钱用在该用的地方。
t_en: The Merchant Guild promises that we will use the money where it is meant to be spent — no disappointments!

t_ch: 另外，这笔钱还会用于码头整体的修缮升级…也就是说，我们暂时无法使用码头物流。
t_en: The funds will be used for the overall repair and upgrade of the dock... which means we won't be able to use the dock's shipping logistics for a while.

t_ch: 为了犒劳你和素裳姑娘长时间以来的操劳，商会在高阿姨那里订了些小吃，你们也正能休息休息，为之后的复兴计划继续做准备。
t_en: In order to reward you and Sushang for your long hours of work, the Merchant Guild has ordered some gourmet snacks from Tall Auntie, so you can rest for a bit and prepare for the revival of Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 小秘书之前跟我打过招呼啦，让我为你们准备些点心，你先去坐下，我一会就端上来。
t_en: Miss Secretary asked me to prepare some refreshments for you earlier. Make yourselves comfortable — I'll bring them out shortly.

t_ch: 我听说了你们为金人巷做了不少好事，慢慢吃，我这边还准备了一些打包带走的。
t_en: I've heard about all the great things you've done for Aurum Alley. Please, take your time! I've packed some for you to take home, too.

t_ch: 抱歉，现在暂时无法使用「码放货物」和「物流规划」。
t_en: I apologize, inventory arrangement and logistics planning are currently unavailable.

t_ch: 商会正在为码头的后续开发计划做准备，等到安全检修结束之后你再来找我吧。
t_en: The Merchant Guild is making preparations for new developments at the dock, so you can come back once the safety check is all said and done.

t_ch: 高阿姨热情地打包了满满一袋外卖给你。
t_en: Tall Auntie enthusiastically packs a full bag of takeout for you.
